findings.description: Logo placement differs between PDF and HTML. In the PDF, the DEQ logo is centered at the top of the page, while in the HTML it is left-aligned.
findings.evidence: PDF: DEQ logo centered at top. HTML: DEQ logo left-aligned at top.

findings.description: The checklist items in the PDF have two columns of checkboxes (YES and NA) preceding each item, but in the HTML, the first item only has one checkbox while items 2-5 have two checkboxes, and items 6-7 have only one checkbox. The checkbox structure is inconsistent and does not uniformly match the PDF's YES/NA dual-checkbox format.
findings.evidence: PDF: Each checklist item has space for YES and NA checkboxes. HTML: Item 1 has one checkbox, items 2-5 have two checkboxes (□ □), items 6-7 have one checkbox (□). The first item about DPLUR compliance only shows one checkbox in HTML instead of two.

findings.description: Inconsistent checkbox rendering across checklist items. The first item has only one checkbox symbol, while items 2-5 have two checkbox symbols. Items 6-7 have only one checkbox symbol. In the original PDF, every item has two columns (YES and NA) for checkboxes.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two checkbox columns (YES and NA) for all 7 items. HTML first item: '☐ All restrictions...' (one checkbox). Items 2-5: '☐ ☐' (two checkboxes). Items 6-7: '☐' (one checkbox). The first item is missing the second checkbox, and items 6-7 are each missing one checkbox.

findings.description: The document title is split across h1 and h2 in the HTML. The PDF has a two-line centered title: 'Inactive Hazardous Sites Branch' and 'Annual Report Form for Perpetual Land-Use Restrictions'. The HTML uses h1 for the first line and h2 for the second, which could imply they are separate sections rather than a unified title.
findings.evidence:
HTML: '

Inactive Hazardous Sites Branch

Annual Report Form for Perpetual Land-Use Restrictions

'. PDF shows these as a single combined title block.

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements — there is an outer <main> in the body and another <main> inside it. This creates structural duplication of the main content wrapper.
findings.evidence: HTML structure: <body><main>...<header>...</header><main>...content...</main></main></body>. The inner <main> is redundant.
